seoTitle: How to Create Buy Button Embeds for Shopify
metaDescription: Sell products outside your Shopify store by embedding buy buttons on any website or blog. Here's how to set them up and where to use them.
intro: Buy button embeds let you turn practically any website or blog into a place customers can purchase from, without requiring them to visit your full Shopify store. This guide covers setting up your first buy button and embedding it into an external site. It's useful for entrepreneurs and small business owners who want to sell products wherever their audience already spends time online.

steps.heading: Set up your Shopify store for buy buttons
steps.body: Make sure your store and products are ready before generating an embeddable buy button.

steps.heading: Create your first buy button
steps.body: Generate a buy button for the specific product you want to sell outside of your main storefront.

steps.heading: Integrate the buy button into your site
steps.body: Embed the button's code into your website or blog so visitors can purchase directly from that page.

steps.heading: Identify the best use cases for buy buttons
steps.body: Consider where embedding a buy button makes the most sense, such as a blog post, landing page, or partner site.

faq.question: Can I use a Shopify buy button on a non-Shopify website?
faq.answer: Yes, buy button embeds are designed to work on external websites and blogs, letting you sell products beyond your main Shopify store.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to add a buy button to my site?
faq.answer: The process is described as straightforward, generating embeddable code you can place on your site without deep technical knowledge.

faq.question: What kinds of products work well with buy button embeds?
faq.answer: The feature suits both handmade crafts and digital products, since it's really about extending your sales reach rather than being limited to one product type.

faq.question: Does a buy button replace my full Shopify store?
faq.answer: No, it complements your store by letting you sell specific products on other sites, rather than replacing your main storefront.
